data.title: David Waal discusses the suitability of Delaware Statutory Trusts (DSTs) as investments, advising to explore if certain criteria fit.
data.description: On January 16, 2026 in San Francisco, a common inquiry revolves around the viability of DSTs as an investment option. The answer to whether DSTs are suitable depends on various factors. Individuals should assess their financial situation and investment goals to determine if DSTs align with their needs. Considering specific criteria can help determine if DSTs are a suitable investment option. It is essential to thoroughly examine DSTs to gauge their compatibility with personal financial strategies.

data.title: RedOne releases album "A.W.A.M." showcasing African culture and unity, featuring artists like Davido and French Montana.
data.description: Renowned music architect RedOne, known for his numerous Grammy awards, has released the star-studded album "A.W.A.M." The album features top artists such as Davido, Ayra Starr, French Montana, Rema, Akon, Ne-Yo, Jason Derulo, and more. The announcement came on January 16, 2026, in Rabat, Morocco. This collaboration brings together a diverse range of musical talents, creating an exciting and unique listening experience for fans worldwide.

data.title: ManpowerGroup addresses AI impact on workforce at Davos, aims to close skills gap. 70% impacted by AI.
data.description: In an effort to bridge the skills gap in the face of an evolving AI landscape, a prominent global workforce leader has joined forces with world leaders to implement practical solutions. The initiative aims to bring a human touch to AI transformation, recognizing the pressing need for upskilling in various industries. This collaboration comes at a crucial time as AI continues to revolutionize the workforce. The announcement was made in Milwaukee on Jan. 16, 2026, underlining the urgency of the situation. With AI's pervasive influence, it is essential to address the increasing demand for skilled professionals. 70% of industries are currently experiencing this critical gap, emphasizing the necessity for immediate action to equip individuals with the relevant skills.

data.title: BTC price at new low vs gold, signaling possible rebound; In late 2022, BTC surged almost 150% post undervaluation.
data.description: The price of BTC experienced a significant increase of around 150% following the detection of an undervaluation signal for BTC compared to gold in late 2022. This surge underscores how substantial discounts have historically been a precursor to significant market upswings.

data.title: Bank of America CEO warns $6 trillion could shift to stablecoins, threatening banks. Stablecoin yields challenging banks' traditional funding sources.
data.description: Brian Moynihan, the CEO of Bank of America (NYSE: BAC), has raised concerns about the potential impact of interest-bearing stablecoins, warning that they could lead to the withdrawal of up to $6 trillion in deposits from banks. During the bank's earnings call, Moynihan highlighted that stablecoins designed similarly to money market funds could attract deposits away from banks, resulting in banks having to rely on more costly wholesale funding instead of the usual inexpensive customer deposits. The central issue is the significant yield difference: with stablecoins offering a 4% yield compared to banks offering a mere 0.1% on savings accounts, depositors are inclined to shift their funds to decentralized platforms. This shift could strip banks of their primary source of cost-effective funding, compelling them to either reduce lending or seek alternative funding sources such as borrowing from the Federal Reserve.

data.title: Legislation by Senator Hildy Angius aims to eliminate financial barriers for breast imaging in Arizona, benefiting thousands.
data.description: In Phoenix, breast cancer organization Susan G. Komen® applauds Senator Hildy Angius (R-Bullhead City) for proposing a bill to improve access to necessary diagnostic and supplementary breast imaging. This legislation aims to remove financial obstacles hindering patients from receiving essential breast cancer screening. The high out-of-pocket expenses linked to this crucial imaging procedure, which can vary from hundreds to thousands of dollars, frequently prevent individuals from accessing the service.

data.title: UDAX Accelerator, a collaboration between Ripple (XRP) and UC Berkeley, graduates 9 startups that tokenized $58M and launched on XRPL mainnet.
data.description: The inaugural cohort of Ripple's UDAX accelerator program has successfully graduated, featuring 9 innovative startups. Among these projects, there are initiatives that have tokenized a total of $58 million in assets. Additionally, these startups have officially been launched on the XRPL mainnet, marking a significant milestone for the program and the participants.

data.title: Five Star Bancorp (Nasdaq: FSBC) declares $0.25/share Q4 cash dividend, 1st increase since April 2023, to be paid on Feb 9.
data.description: A financial company based in Rancho Cordova, California, Five Star Bancorp (Nasdaq: FSBC) declared a cash dividend of $0.25 per share on its common stock. The dividend, the first increase since April 2023, signifies a growth to $0.05 per share. Expected to be paid on February 9, 2026, the dividend record date is set as February 2, 2026. This move reflects the company's robust financial performance, successful organic growth strategy, and dedication to its clients, community partners, and staff.

data.title: TSMC records $16 billion profit with 35% growth, raising price targets, implementing tiered price increases. A fundamental shift in the business model is underway, catering to different customer segments based on AI and smartphone performance.
data.description: Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) reported a remarkable quarter with $16 billion in profit, showing 35% year-over-year growth and aggressive forward guidance. The company is seen as a major player in the AI revolution, leading analysts to raise price targets. However, beneath the surface, TSMC is undergoing a fundamental shift in its business model, with a strategic tweak to pricing where high-performance computing and AI clients face larger price increases than smartphone processor clients. This move reflects TSMC's leverage and its recognition of customer economics. By implementing tiered price increases, TSMC is essentially bifurcating its customer base into those who can afford higher prices and those who are price-sensitive, like Qualcomm and MediaTek. This strategic shift signifies a new complex reality for TSMC as it continues to evolve in the semiconductor industry.
